title: After-Action Report — Decay follow-ups + power-redistribute property tests
scope: The prior AAR parked three follow-up tickets: (1) lineup-cycling drift offset for Decay; (2) selection bias on Q1 Decay — a per-appearance Decay metric would surface this; (3) HR-rate stability check on the redistribute. User: "fix the follow ups". All three shipped.

rationale.item: Lineup-cycling drift offset for Decay: league-wide arc-3 K% will always be higher than arc-1 K% because the 27-out single inning cycles to weaker hitters; use the league's natural arc-1→arc-3 K% delta as the zero-point.
Selection bias on Q1 Decay: low-stamina starters who blow up in arc-2 get pulled before arc-3 — so the cross-arc sample for Q1 is from their best outings, biasing Decay toward zero.

HR-rate stability check: the redistribute is mathematically sum-preserving, but the league HR rate post-change is 2.06% — would need to compare to the exact same seed-set under the old additive model to confirm zero drift.

delegation_events.quote: User: "fix the follow ups". All three shipped.

actions.description: Drift-corrected Decay: _league_baselines() emits league_arc1_k_pct, league_arc3_k_pct, league_decay_drift; _aggregate_pitcher_rows exposes decay_raw and writes decay = decay_raw - league_decay_drift; tooltips updated

Per-appearance Decay + arc-3 reach rate: _pitcher_per_game_decay_map() (one GROUP-BY query against game_pitcher_stats) and _stamp_per_game_decay(rows, drift); new Decay/G and A3% columns on /stats?side=pit&view=all
Property + Monte-Carlo tests for power redistribute: seven tests (sum-preservation hard/medium/weak, identity at zero power_dev, directionality, Monte-Carlo HR-rate stability, per-player spread)
Coefficient bug fix caught by directionality test: POWER_REDIST_HARD_D2T dropped from 0.20 to 0.10 so doubles rise net-positive on positive power

risk_posture: Decay/G for diagnostic comparison only, not as a primary leader-card metric — too noisy at small sample

validation_claims.claim: Drift quantified: league_arc1_k_pct = 22.09%, league_arc3_k_pct = 26.31%, league_decay_drift = -4.22 pp
validation_claims.check_method: Compute baselines on a fresh sim DB

validation_claims.claim: Decay quartile breakdown (drift-corrected) table: e.g. Q1 corrected Decay +3.56, arc3 reach 86.5%; Q4 +2.82, 32.9%
HR-rate stability: HR/PA 2.057% → 2.052% (-0.005pp), 2B/PA 6.434% → 6.523% (+0.089pp), 3B/PA 1.244% → 1.218% (-0.026pp); all deltas under the 1.5pp tolerance asserted by the property test
validation_claims.check_method: Compare pre/post resim rate metrics; run the Monte-Carlo test

validation_claims.claim: Smoke test: 13/13 routes return 200, including the new column layout on /stats?side=pit&view=all

residual_risks: Decay/G runs noisier than season Decay — by a lot (Q1 Decay/G mean +57.55 vs season +3.56); equal-weighting games over-amplifies small samples. Known characteristic to flag (not a bug).
open_hooks: Reading recommendation: Decay for fatigue vs league norm, Decay/G diagnostic only, A3% to know how much the Decay number can be trusted
section_headers_original: After-Action Report — Decay follow-ups + power-redistribute property tests
3. Property + Monte-Carlo tests for power redistribute
Known characteristic to flag (not a bug)
unmapped_content: Header metadata: Branch claude/fix-dark-theme-baseball-terms-7UhIv; predecessor aar-stamina-power-and-latek.md. Interpretation table for drift-corrected Decay (0 = league norm; >0 true fatigue signal; <0 durable arm). arc3_reach_rate is the actual survivor-bias signal: a pitcher with clean Decay = -3 but arc3_reach_rate = 30% has Decay computed from 30% of appearances. Quartile insight: Q1 low-stamina arms post 86.5% reach rate because they're mostly relievers pitching the back of the arc; Q4 workhorses 32.9% because starters get pulled mid-arc — stamina translates to 'can carry a starter workload', not 'completes the arc'. Directionality test detail: at the original D2T = 0.20, single→double gain and double→triple loss cancelled exactly on the doubles row at power_dev=+1; league 2B rate held at 6.52% vs 6.43% prior. Per-render cost note: one query per render, ~18k row GROUP BY. The user's framing (per-appearance Decay would surface the selection bias) holds in spirit — A3% is the cleaner direct surfaced signal, Decay/G the equal-weighted version.
parser_notes: Date from 'Date completed:' line. No explicit non-actions section.
